summary: Reviewed four hydrated low-signal PR sweep candidates. None are boringly clear low-signal closures: each is an XS, focused test portability change with concrete affected files, author-provided Windows proof or ClawSweeper proof labels, and actionable review-bot/ClawSweeper findings that require technical review rather than backlog cleanup.
